metadata.purpose: Central thesis, JAIGP venue alignment, persuasion arc, and strategic decision blocks. This is the strategic layer the section-by-section review loop serves. Annotate with #[COMMENTS] via /section-review level1.
metadata.annotation_note: Everything here was reconstructed from work already done (manuscript at commit 180e2b5, derivation.qmd, the pre-registrations, session memory). Nothing is newly invented. Ratifying it makes it the standard every section is reviewed against; amending it is allowed any time, dated, in decisions.md.
metadata.amended: 2026-06-05: D-09 ratified in-chat - arc restructured from 8-move theory-paper shape to 5-move IMRaD with Methods-as-workflow; methodological thesis component added. Thesis text and D-03/D-04/D-05/D-08 still await annotation.

thesis.primary: Under congenial proper multiple imputation with the complete-data MLE as target, the averaged log-likelihood (the infinite-imputation Q-function) evaluated at the observed-data MLE overstates its complete-data target by +(1/2)tr(RIV) + (A)+(C): an imputation-bias term +tr(RIV) (completions drawn from the fitted imputation model inflate the apparent fit of that same model) minus an estimation-mismatch term (1/2)tr(RIV) (the MI fit targets the complete-data maximizer, not the observed-data one). The result requires an estimated scale or covariance - a known-scale, location-only fit collapses to 0 (deterministic FIML) or -(1/2)tr(RIV) (proper MI) - and under MAR carries an additional O(1) design-imbalance term (A)+(C) that vanishes exactly under MCAR.
thesis.secondary: Likelihood-ratio comparison: when two nested models are fit to the same imputations, the deviance numerator's differential bias at the null is tr(RIV_perp) - the missing information of the tested directions projected in the complete-data metric I_com, provably NOT the naive difference of the two models' own trace corrections, which always overstates it (equality only when the tested directions' missing information decouples, including EFMI and block-decomposing constraints). Sharing imputations collapses the paired noise from O(sqrt n) to O(1) and cancels the (A)+(C) differential exactly at the null; the differential returns at O(1) only for pseudo-true-separated pairs (separation, not nesting, is the criterion). At the null, tr(RIV_perp) is exactly the mean a correctly calibrated reference distribution already absorbs - so the bias matters precisely where no reference stands between the statistic and its use: information-criterion ranking, explicit numerator corrections, and non-nested comparison.
thesis.tertiary: Information-criterion model selection is exactly such a procedure. The bias is model-specific - each candidate's criterion is too small by its own tr(RIV_k) - so uncorrected MI-AIC systematically favors the candidates with the most missing information (100% of misclassifications land on the largest-RIV candidate in the pre-registered congenial cells). Adding tr(RIV_k) repairs the mean and reproduces Shimodaira's AIC_{x;y}; what is new is the anatomy, the MAR term, and the proper-MI scope. Beyond the mean, a pre-registered three-step contrast (mean / two moments / full null distribution) shows the achievable target splits along the null/noncentral axis: the null side calibrates completely (distribution matching selects indistinguishably from complete-data AIC), while no observed-data transform can restore destroyed Fisher information - noncentral statistics shrink by exactly predictable factors, and the weak-signal floor is information loss, not calibration error.
thesis.methodological: The AI-human derivation workflow is itself a contribution, stated as the paper's Methods: preregistration before code with dated amendments and failures reported, a sourced derivation in which every external claim is pinned to a locally archived session-read passage with citation discipline enforced mechanically, a claim pipeline of derive -> dual computer-algebra verification -> Monte Carlo confirmation against frozen pass criteria, cross-model blind re-derivation with an adversarial instructed-to-refute gate, and full reproducibility (seeds, caches, single-entrypoint scripts). The claim: this workflow yields AI-assisted mathematical results defensible at the standard a skeptical referee applies to human-derived work - and the Results section is the demonstration, with every claim carrying its trust tier (firm / measured / structural / conjecture) and the gates it cleared. The fitted-vs-oracle sign episode (8 of 9 blind re-derivations landing on the opposite sign until the conditioning was pinned) is the worked example of why the verification gates exist.
thesis.venue_alignment: JAIGP submission: an AI-assisted derivation with an ORCID-verified human prompter, written for an AI-skeptical academic audience. The credibility case is itself part of the contribution: every external claim pinned to a locally archived, session-read source; every number traced to a committed pre-registered artifact, with failed predictions reported; load-bearing claims cross-checked by an independent model lineage under a blind adversarial protocol; citation discipline enforced mechanically. Claims are trust-ordered throughout - firm (proved + verified) first, measurements next, conjectures last and labeled.

persuasion_arc.design_note: IMRaD adapted to an AI-human derivation (D-09): the workflow that makes the claims defensible is the Methods, not end-matter. The reader evaluates the Results through the Methods, as in empirical work. Within Results the internal order stays theorem -> LRT -> IC -> numerics (D-03 unamended).

moves.label: INTRODUCTION
moves.title: Introduction
moves.function: Two-layer opening. Scientific layer: the averaged log-likelihood is not an unbiased stand-in for the complete-data log-likelihood; headline bias +(1/2)tr(RIV) + (A)+(C); the two applications; positioning upstream of the Chan calibration line and against the model-selection lineage; what is new and what is not. Meta layer: this is an AI-human derivation under a defensibility protocol, the paper reports both the results and the workflow, and the Methods section is where that workflow is stated.
key_claims: Deviances built from the averaged log-likelihood are systematically too favorable, by an exactly characterized amount
Calibration (Chan line) makes the reference right; this work makes the statistic referred to it centered - complementary
The trace penalty is Shimodaira's AIC_{x;y}; new: the decomposition, the MAR term, proper-MI scope, and the two application-level results
The derivation is AI-assisted under a stated verification protocol; the workflow is a contribution, reported as Methods
moves.rhetorical_role: State the whole result and the trust framework in the first two pages.

moves.label: BACKGROUND
moves.title: Background
moves.function: Restate each standard result in its source's own terms at the precision later steps need - the EM rate matrix is NOT the RIV, Rubin's rules are exact given one named approximation, observed and expected information differ under MAR - each restatement naming the proposition that depends on it (D-04).
key_claims: Ignorability; EM/Q-function; missing-information principle; Rubin's rules as exact posterior-moment identities; MCEM bridge; congeniality; AIC as bias-corrected plug-in; Wilks; observed-vs-expected information under MAR; MI test combining and calibration
moves.rhetorical_role: Pre-empt the standard misreadings before they can occur.

moves.label: METHODS
moves.title: Methods: the derivation and verification workflow
moves.function: State the AI-human workflow as method, in six parts: (1) roles and decision discipline - human prompter of record (direction, gates, decisions recorded with rejected options), AI derivation, dated decision log; (2) sourced derivation - every external claim pinned to a source passage in a locally archived session-read copy, citation discipline enforced mechanically by a pre-write hook; (3) claim pipeline - derive, then symbolic verification in two independent computer-algebra systems, then Monte Carlo confirmation, with trust tiers (firm / measured / structural / conjecture) assigned by the gates cleared; (4) preregistration before code - predictions and pass criteria frozen and committed first, dated amendments, failures reported; (5) cross-model adversarial verification - blind re-derivation by an independent model lineage plus an instructed-to-refute pass, grading records committed, with the fitted-vs-oracle sign episode as the worked example of what this gate catches; (6) reproducibility - seeds, caches, single-entrypoint scripts, environment records.
key_claims: Each verification gate is stated with what it can and cannot catch
Trust tiers used throughout Results are defined here, once

The fitted-vs-oracle episode: 8 of 9 blind re-derivations produced the opposite sign until the conditioning was pinned - the protocol caught a sign error a single-lineage derivation would likely have shipped
Failed preregistered predictions are part of the record, not the supplement
moves.rhetorical_role: Answer 'why believe an AI derivation' before any result is asked to be believed.

moves.label: RESULTS
moves.title: Results
moves.function: The output of the Methods, graded by its trust tiers, in four subsections kept in the current internal order (D-03 unamended): (a) setup and main theorem - one RIV, fitted-vs-oracle stated up front, the A/B split, the (A)+(C) term with its three-tier standing, known-scale collapse, entropy-plug-in consistency check; (b) likelihood-ratio comparison - tr(RIV_perp) in the I_com metric, the naive-difference overstatement with exact gap, the paired-noise collapse, the null cancellation and its return under separation, the absorption argument; (c) information-criterion selection - corrected criterion = AIC_{x;y}, directional misclassification signature, the calibration ladder with the null/noncentral split and the two exact structural limits; (d) the pre-registered numerical studies - apparatus once, findings including the misses, engine sensitivity scoped.
key_claims: E[T] = +(1/2)tr(RIV) + (A)+(C); pooled MC 2.43 +/- 0.26 vs augmented range 2.42-2.55
LRT differential = tr(RIV_perp) in the I_com metric; naive always overstates (52 se discrimination); pairing O(1); null cancellation exact
IC: misclassifications land on the largest-RIV candidate; rungs 2-3 reach the oracle on the null side (0.904/0.903 vs 0.899); noncentral shrinkage at predicted factors; weak-signal floor 0.17
Failures reported: similar-pair differential below resolution; N=1000 cell at 2.6 se; component-level 9-20% misses
moves.rhetorical_role: Every claim arrives pre-graded; the reader never has to guess a claim's standing.

moves.label: DISCUSSION
moves.title: Discussion and conclusion
moves.function: Scope and limits G1-G6, each next to the claim it qualifies; the power-dominance conjecture stated as a conjecture with partial support; three directions (covariate shift per Shimodaira's named future work, exact-engine replication, selection-aware calibration developed separately); and a short reflection on the workflow itself - what the gates caught, what they cost, what they cannot catch. Ends with the formal JAIGP provenance declaration (the former standalone section, shrunk).
key_claims: The workflow reflection is itself trust-ordered: what is demonstrated about the protocol vs what is asserted
moves.rhetorical_role: Explicit limits as the closing trust move; the declaration is a formality because the whole paper performed it.

decisions.label: Frame as deviance-bias correction upstream of test calibration
decisions.chosen: The paper derives the bias of the deviance numerator; Chan (2022, AoS) is cited as the canonical reference for MI test calibration. Complementarity stated explicitly: calibration makes the reference right, this work makes the statistic centered.

rejected_options.spectral_correction_headline: The original spectral chi-square correction / generalized eigendecomposition framing - preempted by Chan (2022, AoS), which already carries the full eigenvalue spectrum and MC reference. Archived in IDEAS.md; do not draft from it.
rejected_options.compete_with_chan: Positioning the correction as an alternative to Chan's calibration would be both wrong (they compose) and strategically fatal (his machinery is more general for testing).

decisions.label: Headline sign convention: fitted-imputation Q-bar-infinity
decisions.chosen: The theorem is stated for the FITTED-imputation Q-function (+1/2 tr(RIV)), with the oracle-conditioning contrast (-1/2, by the tower property) stated prominently inside the setup section, and the sign-reversal experiment cited as direct confirmation.

rejected_options.oracle_definition: Defining Q-bar-infinity as E[l_com | Y_obs] under the data-generating law flips the headline to -1/2 - mathematically coherent but not what any MI procedure computes; 8 of 9 blind re-derivations landed there when the conditioning was left implicit.
rejected_options.suppress_the_contrast: Omitting the fitted-vs-oracle discussion invites exactly that blind-derivation error in referees.

decisions.provenance: todo/020; sec-qfun callout in derivation.qmd; memory project_fitted_vs_oracle_sign

decisions.label: Evidence placement: consolidated numerics section plus inline headline numbers
decisions.chosen: One consolidated pre-registered-evidence section (sec-numerics) holding the apparatus, the grading, and the failures; headline numbers ALSO quoted inline at the point of each claim (e.g. 52 se, 0.904 vs 0.899, -1.2 at 3.5 se). This is the analogue of SAMPLE-STRATEGY-ECD's threading decision, adapted: their evidence was one dataset serving rhetoric; ours is six pre-registered studies whose amendment histories must be readable as a unit.

rejected_options.full_threading: Distributing the studies into the application sections fragments the pre-registration narrative (predictions, amendments, failures) that JAIGP provenance requires to be auditable in one place.
rejected_options.numerics_only: Keeping all numbers in sec-numerics leaves sections 3-5 floating without evidence at the point of claim.

decisions.note_d09: Under D-09 the consolidated evidence account becomes the closing Results subsection; the placement choice (consolidated account + inline headline numbers at point of claim) is unchanged and still proposed. Marcus did not amend it when ratifying D-09.

decisions.label: Background as precision restatement, not citation list
decisions.chosen: sec-background restates each standard result in its source's own terms, with displays, each restatement naming the proposition that later depends on it (B1-B11 background layer from the argument map).

rejected_options.cite_only: A conventional citation paragraph invites the two standard conflations (RIV vs EM rate matrix; naive vs unconditional information) that the derivation spends real effort preventing.
rejected_options.appendix_background: Moving it to an appendix breaks the arc - M3 needs the reader already inoculated.

decisions.label: Trust-ordered claims with failures reported
decisions.chosen: Claims are graded firm / measured / structural in the text wherever standing differs ((A)+(C) three-tier paragraph; ladder limits; G1-G6), and failed predictions are reported in the main text (similar-pair headline NOT met; N=1000 cell at 2.6 se; component-level misses).

rejected_options.headline_first: Leading with the strongest numbers and footnoting the misses reads as salesmanship to exactly the referee this venue attracts.
rejected_options.failures_in_supplement: Hiding failures in supplementary material contradicts the provenance posture the paper itself argues for.

decisions.provenance: standing outside-reader feedback; W4 reporting in mi-spectral.qmd

decisions.label: In-paper vs sequel scope split
decisions.chosen: In-paper: theorem + LRT application + IC application including calibration-ladder rungs 1-3 (mean / two-moment / equipercentile on saturated-anchored statistics). Sequel: de-shrinkage of estimated noncentrality, Z-testbed, copula/triangle designs, joint calibration of score vectors.

rejected_options.everything_in_one: Length and dilution; the sequel material is a different contribution (selection-aware calibration) with its own apparatus.
rejected_options.rungs_to_sequel: The original todo/023 split sent the ladder to the sequel; reversed 2026-06-04 (todo/026 rev 2) because the rungs are the null-side completeness result - without them the IC application stops at the mean and undersells the split.

decisions.provenance: todo/023, todo/026 rev 2, todo/027; memory project_scope_split_2026_06_03

decisions.label: General statement, multivariate-normal verification
decisions.chosen: The theorem is stated for general regular likelihoods with an estimated scale; every constant is verified (symbolically and by simulation) in the normal family only, and G6 says so. No general-family verification is attempted in this paper.

rejected_options.mvn_only_statement: Stating the theorem only for the normal family undersells the mechanism (the entropy-Hessian split is general).
rejected_options.chase_general_verification: Verifying constants beyond the normal family is open-ended and delays submission without changing the contribution's standing.

decisions.label: Companion-document posture
decisions.chosen: The paper is self-contained at the level of statements and evidence; manuscript/derivation.qmd carries full proofs, the source-passage audit trail, and executable symbolic verification, and is cited as the companion throughout.

rejected_options.inline_full_proofs: Doubles the length and buries the applications; the proof detail is auditable in the companion.
rejected_options.pure_pointer: Stating results with no proof sketches (Term B Taylor argument, the I_com-metric reasoning, the Schur gap) would make the paper un-refereeable standalone.

decisions.provenance: draft-status callout in mi-spectral.qmd; derivation-audit PASS at 180e2b5

decisions.label: Paper macro-structure: IMRaD with Methods-as-workflow
decisions.chosen: Introduction / Background / Methods / Results / Discussion-Conclusion. Methods states the AI-human derivation-and-verification workflow as method (roles and decision discipline; sourced derivation with mechanical citation enforcement; derive -> dual-CAS -> Monte Carlo pipeline with trust tiers; preregistration before code with amendments and reported failures; cross-model blind plus adversarial verification, fitted-vs-oracle episode as worked example; reproducibility). Results = setup+theorem, LRT, IC, preregistered numerics, in that order. A fourth, methodological thesis component is added. Rationale: the AI-human derivation is itself a new contribution and the venue's central question is 'why believe an AI derivation' - the workflow is where claims become strongly defensible, so it is Methods, not end-matter.

rejected_options.theory_paper_structure: The 180e2b5 draft structure (problem -> background -> theorem -> applications -> numerics -> discussion -> provenance statement): buries the workflow in an end-of-paper statement, underselling the venue's central question.
rejected_options.workflow_in_supplement: A pure results paper with the protocol in supplementary material: contradicts the trust posture the paper itself argues for.

metrics_gate.notes: Five IMRaD moves (D-09). M3 METHODS serves the methodological thesis; M1/M5 serve all components. Section anchors sec-methods and sec-results do not exist in the qmd yet - they are targets for the Level-2 blueprint and the restructuring decomposition. Gate re-evaluated at every Level-1 amendment.
